description: One-pass review of crate secretx-local-signing (secretx-local-signing/). Filed by /review-rusty-crate.
notes:
Maintainer risk: LOW — crate is 504 LOC including tests, clippy-clean at default level, and well-commented. The cryptographic primitives are all from RustCrypto with no hand-rolled crypto. Constructor does eager validation and zeroizes raw key buffer.

Correctness confidence: ~85% chance of NO missed bug. Highest-risk finding is std::fs::read non-zeroizing buffer growth (parallel to qth2.3 in secretx-file) — already a known issue pattern in this workspace. Uncertainty driven by: I have not run a memory-scrub test to confirm leftover key bytes survive in deallocated heap pages after construction.

Idiom rating: GOOD — code is idiomatic, comments explain non-obvious choices (the Arc<RsaPss SigningKey> for spawn_blocking, the UFCS for SigningKey::as_ref disambiguation, the two-stage validate_algorithm/parse_key separation). Findings are mostly workspace-consistency items (Debug derive, unknown-param rejection) plus pedantic clippy hygiene.

Rust idiom confidence: ~85% chance of NO missed issue. Least confident: whether mandatory ?algorithm= param vs AWS-KMS's defaultable param is a deliberate consistency call or accidental drift — leaving as a doc cross-reference rather than an API change.

Cross-crate gating: unknown-param rejection (filed below) is gated by avkc.4 (SecretUri params not yet iterable).
title: [epic] Review: secretx-local-signing rusty pass
